item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my take as well.

There are a number of elements that read like they are meant to set up ideas for other tales or SCP's (Penrose, linguistic laylines), but they come across as insubstantial gestures in the direction of a mystery, rather than thought-out plot points.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Neither image could be sourced reliably and are assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, both images have since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unsurprisingly, this article has not been added to the Russian translation branch. I wouldn't be surprised if it never was, given its subject matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doing it for the purpose of being obvious I can understand for say, a few places here and there, but every front company? That's just showing the GOC and other gois your entire deck. They would know everywhere you are. After all, the only people who it would be immediately obvious for are people who already know about the foundation. To civilians, they look at it and go "Huh, I wonder why there are so many companies that abriviate to SCP". A few may go looking for information and wind up looking towards sources of sketchy legality at best if they don't chalk it up to coincidence and are a conspiracy theorist, and eventually come upon the hidden organizations.

As for my second point I find it pointless and honestly a little annoying when it is just a random s.c.p. because why not. I mean the example I provided in my previous comment could have just as easily been anything else. I mean what does it matter whether it's the original or an exact replica when it comes to containment? Either way you have a car appearing in the location. And how is it even a containment proposal? It is just a theory about how it works not how to contain it!

Anyway, enough of my rambling. I should probably be getting to bed. It's midnight for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of people headcanon that they do the S.C.P. thing on purpose for various reasons- I fall into the "they do it with company names when want it to obviously be a Foundation front so no one screws with it" camp myself. There's also a theory floating around that the Foundation has a sympathetic magic thing going with SCP that reinforces containment and protects the organization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation shell group, Sawgrass Holdings

Finally a front company that isn't S__ C__ P__. I mean seriously you'd think the foundation never learned the meaning of discreet.

Edit:

4. "Or an exact replica of the same." - Secondary Containment Proposal

Goshdarnit, why must every skip have something that abbreviates to SCP? (Aside from the SCP number and special containment procedures of course)

Aside from that, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The addendum after the interview is completely unnecessary, as far as I can tell.

And like… yeah, they realized that magic existed and used it to make a few bucks, but wtf else were they supposed to do with an anomaly that steals cars for them? Build a cult out of it? Use their total lack of relevant knowledge to fail to make more magic things? This anomaly wasn't so terribly fantastic that these car thieves could have ruined it, anyways.

I don't really buy into the message of the article, and the literal content of the article doesn't do nearly enough to carry it in lieu of that. -1.

Oh, and the whole "linguistic leyline" thing feels gimmicky when it's not backed up by much of anything.

item.title: Congrats on +50!
item.content:encoded: An interesting (and tastefully comedic) SCP. Solowski's line at the end is just pure gold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny, really, these guys find out 'magic' is out there and their first thought is stealing cars. People really aren't that creative, you know?

Upvoted because I know a disturbing number of people that would either have this exact sort of reaction, or find something to do with it involving alcoholic beverages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The characterizations are quite good, and the idea is excellent. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the last lines of the interview, and the PC tone of that final footnote really tickles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As always, impeccable work! One minor quibble:
…we ran it up the latter?

Edit: I really shouldn't read, watch TV, and then come back and comment without refreshing…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted at "and, in one instance, an airboat."

I'd kind of like to write a local SCP somewhere around DNM, but Huntsville is more rockets and Nazis than voodoo and revivals :(.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah we did our manners proud
On the night we said goodbye.
No big scenes out there on the sidewalk
As we pinpointed the place where all good things go to die.
— Somebody Else's Parking Lot In Sebastopol, The Extra Glenns

New SCP in the world of Dixieland Nightmare Magic. It's amazing how mundane the fantastic becomes in the wrong hands.
